  "text": "The CronJob v1beta1 to batch/v1 migration in Kubernetes involves several layers of Go source files across multiple repositories.\n\n## kubernetes--v1.32.0\n\n### Internal API Types\n- `pkg/apis/batch/types.go`: Internal (hub) representation of CronJob including the `TimeZone *string` field in `CronJobSpec`.\n\n### Conversion (v1beta1 <-> internal)\n- `pkg/apis/batch/v1beta1/conversion.go`: Manual conversion registration via `addConversionFuncs`, sets up field label converters for CronJob.\n- `pkg/apis/batch/v1beta1/zz_generated.conversion.go`: Auto-generated functions `Convert_v1beta1_CronJob_To_batch_CronJob`, `Convert_batch_CronJob_To_v1beta1_CronJob`, `autoConvert_v1beta1_CronJob_To_batch_CronJob`, `autoConvert_v1beta1_CronJobSpec_To_batch_CronJobSpec`, `Convert_v1beta1_CronJobSpec_To_batch_CronJobSpec`, and `Convert_v1beta1_CronJobList_To_batch_CronJobList`.\n\n### Conversion (v1 <-> internal)\n- `pkg/apis/batch/v1/conversion.go`: Manual conversion registration for v1 Job (field label conversions).\n- `pkg/apis/batch/v1/zz_generated.conversion.go`: Auto-generated `Convert_v1_CronJob_To_batch_CronJob`, `Convert_batch_CronJob_To_v1_CronJob`, `autoConvert_v1_CronJob_To_batch_CronJob`, `autoConvert_v1_CronJobSpec_To_batch_CronJobSpec`, `Convert_v1_CronJobSpec_To_batch_CronJobSpec`.\n\n### Defaulting Logic\n- `pkg/apis/batch/v1/defaults.go`: `SetDefaults_CronJob` for batch/v1 — sets `ConcurrencyPolicy`, `Suspend`, `SuccessfulJobsHistoryLimit`, and `FailedJobsHistoryLimit`.\n- `pkg/apis/batch/v1beta1/defaults.go`: `SetDefaults_CronJob` for v1beta1 — similar logic but for the older API version.\n- `pkg/apis/batch/v1/zz_generated.defaults.go` and `pkg/apis/batch/v1beta1/zz_generated.defaults.go`: Generated `SetObjectDefaults_CronJob` wrappers that chain `SetDefaults_CronJob` with Pod spec defaulting.\n\n### Validation\n- `pkg/apis/batch/validation/validation.go`: `ValidateCronJobCreate`, `ValidateCronJobUpdate`, `validateCronJobSpec` — validates schedule, TimeZone (checked against the tz database), concurrency policy, and job template. The `strategy.go` also emits warnings if TZ/CRON_TZ is used in schedule instead of the `timeZone` field.\n\n### Registry/Strategy\n- `pkg/registry/batch/cronjob/strategy.go`: CronJob storage strategy (PrepareForCreate, PrepareForUpdate, Validate, etc.), including the warning about `timeZone` vs schedule TZ usage.\n- `pkg/registry/batch/cronjob/storage/storage.go`: REST storage for CronJob.\n\n### Staging (external-facing API types)\n- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/batch/v1/types.go`: Public batch/v1 CronJob types with `TimeZone *string` in `CronJobSpec`.\n- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/batch/v1beta1/types.go`: Public batch/v1beta1 CronJob types, also with `TimeZone *string` (backported).\n- Apply configuration helpers with `WithTimeZone` in both v1 and v1beta1.\n\n## api--v0.32.0\n- `batch/v1/types.go` and `batch/v1beta1/types.go`: Public API type definitions.\n- `batch/v1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go` and `batch/v1beta1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go`: DeepCopy handling for `TimeZone *string`.\n\n## client-go--v0.32.0\n- `applyconfigurations/batch/v1/cronjobspec.go`: Apply configuration with `WithTimeZone`.\n- `applyconfigurations/batch/v1beta1/cronjobspec.go`: Same for v1beta1.\n- `kubernetes/typed/batch/v1/cronjob.go` and `kubernetes/typed/batch/v1beta1/cronjob.go`: Client interfaces for both versions.\n\nThe key migration difference: batch/v1 is the stable GA version with `TimeZone` as an officially supported field, while v1beta1 was deprecated (removed in 1.25) as a replacement. Conversion between the two goes through the internal representation. The `TimeZone` field was present in v1beta1 as well (backported), so the conversion is straightforward. The main differences in defaulting are minor (same logic applies to both). Validation in `validateCronJobSpec` validates the `TimeZone` value against the IANA tz database for both versions."
}

including CronJobSpec.\n\n### Staging (external-facing API types)\n- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/batch/v1/types.go`: Public batch/v1 CronJob types with `TimeZone *string` in `CronJobSpec`.\n- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/batch/v1beta1/types.go`: Public batch/v1beta1 CronJob types, also with `TimeZone *string` (backported from v1).\n- Apply configuration helpers with `WithTimeZone` in both v1 and v1beta1.\n\n## api--v0.32.0\n- `batch/v1/types.go` and `batch/v1beta1/types.go`: Public API type definitions.\n- `batch/v1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go` and `batch/v1beta1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go`: DeepCopy handling for `TimeZone *string`.\n- `batch/v1/types_swagger_doc_generated.go` and `batch/v1beta1/types_swagger_doc_generated.go`: Swagger docs with TimeZone documentation.\n\n## client-go--v0.32.0\n- `applyconfigurations/batch/v1/cronjobspec.go`: Apply configuration with `WithTimeZone`.\n- `applyconfigurations/batch/v1beta1/cronjobspec.go`: Same for v1beta1.\n- `kubernetes/typed/batch/v1/cronjob.go` and `kubernetes/typed/batch/v1beta1/cronjob.go`: Client interfaces for both versions.\n\nThe key migration difference: batch/v1 is the stable GA version with `TimeZone` as an officially supported field, while v1beta1 was deprecated (removed in 1.25 as replacement for v1). Conversion between the two versions goes through the internal representation. The `TimeZone` field was backported to v1beta1 as well, so the conversion is straightforward. Validation in `validateCronJobSpec` validates the `TimeZone` value against the IANA tz database for both versions, and the strategy layer warns when TZ timezone notation is used in the schedule cron expression instead of the explicit `timeZone` field."
